w:r.w:t: Full Stack Mini Instagram Clone | Week 3 – Task

InstaConnect is a production-quality, full-stack social media web application modeled after Instagram. It enables users to share photos, discover content, engage through likes and comments, and manage personal profiles. The application is built for an internship Week 3 capstone task and serves as a reference-quality codebase demonstrating clean MVC architecture, RESTful API design, and component-driven UI development.
InstaConnect proves that a feature-rich social platform can be engineered with elegant simplicity, professional code quality, and a premium user experience — without sacrificing engineering rigour.
Most Instagram-clone projects available as learning resources share a set of systemic weaknesses:
• Inconsistent architecture that breaks under real load conditions.
• Security shortcuts — plain-text passwords, missing SQL injection protection.
• Amateur-quality UIs with no loading states, empty states, or error handling.
• Missing essential social features: follow system, notifications, infinite scroll.
• Insufficient documentation, forcing new developers to guess intent.
InstaConnect solves all of these problems by delivering a fully documented, architecturally sound, security-hardened, premium-UI social platform.
• Deliver a secure, performant, and scalable social media platform with core Instagram-like features.
• Implement industry-standard JWT authentication with bcrypt password hashing (12 salt rounds).
• Enable image post creation, management, and sharing via Cloudinary CDN.
• Provide a real-time-feeling interactive feed with infinite scroll and optimistic UI updates.
• Allow users to follow/unfollow each other, building a personalised content feed.
• Implement robust search for discovering other users.
• Maintain strict MVC architecture on the backend and component-driven architecture on the frontend.
• Ensure full responsiveness across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints.
• Achieve a Lighthouse performance score of 85+ on desktop.
The architecture is strictly MVC and must never change. All future modules must integrate with this established architecture.
• General public aged 16–35 interested in sharing photos and connecting socially.
• Content creators sharing personal or professional visual content.
• Individuals seeking to follow and engage with creators they admire.
• Software development interns and junior engineers using the codebase as a learning reference.
• Internship evaluators assessing code quality, architecture, and feature completeness.
• Technical reviewers grading the Week 3 capstone submission.
— Secure signup, login, logout with bcrypt-hashed passwords and JWT sessions.
— Upload images with captions; stored in MySQL, images hosted on Cloudinary.
— Display all followed-user posts in a dynamic, infinite-scroll responsive feed.
— Like and unlike posts with real-time count updates and optimistic UI.
— Add, view, and delete comments on posts with timestamps and user avatars.
— User profile with avatar, bio, stats (posts/followers/following), and post grid.
— Follow or unfollow any user; follower and following counts update instantly.
— Real-time user search by username or full name with recent search history.
— Bell icon with unread badge; list of like, comment, and follow notifications.
— Update full name, username, bio, and profile picture via Cloudinary.
— Toggle between light and dark theme; preference persisted in localStorage.
— Save posts to a private bookmarks collection accessible from the profile.
— Live notification delivery via WebSockets (Socket.io) without page refresh.
— Full production deployment — GitHub repo + live demo URL submitted.
I can create a personal account and start sharing
I can access my personalised feed and profile
upload an image with a caption
I can share my moments with followers
scroll an infinite feed of posts
I can discover new content without clicking through pages
I can show appreciation for content I enjoy
add and delete comments on posts
I can engage in conversation around content
my feed shows content from people I care about
view any user's profile and post grid
I can learn about other users and their content
my profile reflects my current identity
search for users by username or name
I can discover and follow new people
I stay informed about engagement on my content
I can remove content I no longer want to share
I can use the app comfortably in low-light environments
I can save content to revisit later
I can keep my account secure
• Collect: full name, username, email, password.
• Validate all fields before submission (see Section 11 — Validation Rules).
• Hash password with bcrypt (12 salt rounds); never store plain text.
• Return JWT access token (7-day expiry) on success.
• Reject duplicate email (HTTP 409) and duplicate username (HTTP 409) with specific messages.
• Accept email + password. Verify against stored hash.
• Return JWT + user data on success. Return generic 401 on failure (no email enumeration).
• Rate-limit to 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP.
• Generate a cryptographically secure 32-byte hex token, hash it, store with 1-hour expiry.
• Email the raw token as a reset link to the user.
• Validate token on reset, hash new password, delete used token.
• Every protected API endpoint requires Authorization: Bearer {token} header.
• authMiddleware verifies token; attaches req.user; returns 401 on failure.
• Frontend Next.js middleware guards all routes except /login, /register, /forgot-password, /reset-password.
• Create post: single image upload (JPG/PNG/WEBP, max 10 MB) + optional caption (max 2,200 chars).
• Multi-step upload modal: (1) image select + drag-drop, (2) crop with aspect ratio choice, (3) caption entry.
• Client-side compression: downscale images > 1920 px before upload; re-encode at JPEG 0.85 quality.
• Backend: Multer receives file → Cloudinary upload with auto quality + format optimisation → save URL + public_id to DB.
• Edit caption: post owners can update caption via PATCH /api/posts/{postId}.
• Delete post: post owner or ADMIN; triggers Cloudinary asset deletion; confirmation modal required.
• View post: post detail modal (desktop) or full page /posts/{postId} (mobile).
• Toggle like/unlike via heart icon. POST /api/posts/{postId}/like — DELETE to unlike.
• Unique constraint (userId, postId) prevents duplicate likes.
• Optimistic UI: heart icon and count update immediately; revert on API failure.
• Heart animates: scale 1.0 → 1.3 → 1.0 on like (300 ms spring).
• Add comment: POST /api/posts/{postId}/comments with body (1–2,200 chars).
• Delete own comment; post owner can delete any comment on their post.
• Load 20 comments initially; 'Load more' for subsequent pages.
• Comment count badge on post cards; updates optimistically.
• Users cannot follow themselves (enforced at application layer).
• Unique constraint (followerId, followeeId) prevents duplicate follows.
• Follower / following counts on profiles update in real time.
• Feed shows posts from all followed users, sorted by createdAt DESC (cursor-based pagination).
• Real-time search with 300 ms debounce. Minimum 1 character triggers the search.
• Results show avatar, username, full name, follower count, and a Follow button.
• Last 10 searches stored in localStorage; shown on empty focus; clearable individually or all at once.
• Types: LIKE, COMMENT, FOLLOW — stored in notifications table.
• Unread count badge on bell icon; fetched on page load.
• Mark all as read when Notifications page is visited.
API response time for standard GET endpoints
Pool min 5 / max 20; horizontal scaling ready
100% parameterised via mysql2 — no string concatenation
WCAG 2.1 AA — minimum 4.5:1 for normal text
Page-specific <title> and <meta description> on every page
Chrome · Firefox · Safari · Edge — latest stable
320 px – 1440 px+; no horizontal scroll at any width

InstaConnect follows a "Focused Clarity" design philosophy: every visual element earns its place by serving the content. Photos are the heroes; the UI is their elegant frame. The interface is premium, minimal, and content-forward.
• Login / Register — Centred card layout, max-width 350 px, borderless on mobile.
• Feed — Sidebar (desktop) + centred post list (max 470 px) + suggestions panel (desktop).
• Post Detail — Left: full image. Right: header, comments, action bar (desktop modal). Stacked on mobile.
• Profile — Avatar (150 px), stats row, bio, 3-column post grid.
• Search — Dropdown overlay (desktop); full-page with explore grid (mobile).
• Notifications — Grouped by time; unread highlighted with left blue border.
• Settings — Left settings nav + content panel; Change Password, Account, Dark Mode, Privacy.
• Typography: System font stack (-apple-system, Segoe UI, Helvetica…); scale from 11 px body to 28 px display.
• Spacing: 4 px base unit; all values multiples of 4.
• Animations: 150 ms default transitions; like heart burst 300 ms spring; modal open scale 0.95 → 1.0.
• Dark Mode: Tailwind 'class' strategy; toggled on <html>; persisted in localStorage.
• Skeletons: Shimmer animation on all loading states matching real component dimensions.
• Toasts: Bottom-right desktop / bottom-centre mobile; auto-dismiss 4 s; max 3 stacked.
• Accessibility: All interactive elements keyboard navigable; focus trapping in modals; ARIA labels on all icons.
Required · 8–128 chars · upper + lower + digit + special
Password must contain uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
Username can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
Name can only contain letters and spaces.
Only JPG, PNG, and WEBP images are allowed.
Profile picture must not exceed 5 MB.
MySQL 8.0+. Normalised to 3NF. All foreign keys indexed. Cascade deletes maintain referential integrity.
• posts — id · userId (FK) · imageUrl · imagePublicId · caption · createdAt · updatedAt
• likes — id · userId (FK) · postId (FK) · createdAt | UNIQUE (userId, postId)
• comments — id · postId (FK) · userId (FK) · parentCommentId (FK, nullable) · body · createdAt
• follows — id · followerId (FK) · followeeId (FK) · createdAt | UNIQUE (followerId, followeeId)
• notifications — id · recipientUserId (FK) · senderUserId (FK) · type · postId · commentId · isRead · createdAt
• password_reset_tokens — id · userId (FK) · tokenHash · expiresAt · createdAt
Base URL: /api | All responses: { success, data, message } | Auth: Bearer token
• SQL Injection — All queries use parameterised statements via mysql2; no string concatenation in SQL.
• XSS — React JSX escapes all user content by default; no dangerouslySetInnerHTML used.
• CSRF — JWT Bearer token authentication is inherently CSRF-resistant.
• Rate Limiting — Login: 5 req / 15 min per IP · Registration: 3 req / hr · Uploads: 10 / hr per user.
• HTTP Headers — Helmet.js enforces CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options: DENY, HSTS.
• File Upload — MIME type validated via magic bytes (file-type package); files stored in /tmp and deleted immediately after Cloudinary upload.
• CORS — Origin restricted to FRONTEND_URL environment variable; no wildcard (*) in production.
• Secrets — All credentials in .env files; .env never committed to version control.
The following steps describe the end-to-end image upload process for a post:
• Step 1 — Client selects image; frontend validates MIME type and file size.
• Step 2 — Client compresses image: downscale if > 1920 px; re-encode as JPEG @ 0.85 quality.
• Step 3 — Cropped canvas exported as Blob; wrapped in FormData (field: 'image').
• Step 5 — Multer receives file; validates MIME via fileFilter; saves to /tmp/uploads/.
• Step 6 — Controller calls cloudinaryUpload(filePath) → folder: instaconnect/posts, width: 1080, quality: auto, format: auto.
• Step 7 — Cloudinary returns secure_url and public_id.
• Step 8 — cleanupUpload deletes temp file from /tmp/uploads/.
• Step 9 — Post record (imageUrl, imagePublicId, caption, userId) inserted into MySQL.
• Step 10 — 201 response with post object returned to client.
Error handling: if Cloudinary fails, temp file is deleted and 500 returned. If DB insert fails after successful Cloudinary upload, the Cloudinary asset is deleted to prevent orphans.
• src/app/ — Next.js 13 App Router pages. (auth) group = unauthenticated; (main) group = authenticated with layout.
• src/components/ — Reusable React components grouped by feature: auth, layout, feed, post, comment, upload, profile, search, notification, settings, ui.
• src/hooks/ — Custom hooks: useAuth, usePosts, useLike, useComments, useFollow, useSearch, useInfiniteScroll, useTheme, useToast.
• src/services/ — API call functions per resource (authService, postService, likeService…).
• src/utils/ — Pure utility functions: dateUtils, imageUtils, tokenUtils, validationUtils, numberUtils.
• src/controllers/ — MVC controllers: auth, user, post, like, comment, notification.
• src/models/ — MVC models: User, Post, Like, Comment, Follow, Notification, PasswordResetToken.
• src/routes/ — Route definitions mapped to controllers via Express Router.
• src/middleware/ — authMiddleware, adminMiddleware, uploadMiddleware, rateLimiter, errorHandler, notFoundHandler.
• src/validators/ — express-validator rule sets per resource.
• src/utils/ — generateToken, hashPassword, cloudinaryUpload, cloudinaryDelete, apiResponse, paginationHelper.
• tmp/uploads/ — Temporary Multer storage; files auto-deleted after Cloudinary upload.
Auth system · MySQL schema · Sidebar & Navbar · Protected routes · JWT middleware
Post creation · Cloudinary upload · Feed (infinite scroll) · Likes · Comments · Post Detail modal
Follow system · Profile pages · Edit Profile · Search · Notifications · Explore grid
Dark mode · Settings · Skeletons · Error boundaries · Responsive polish · Tests · Production deploy
• All validators — every rule tested with valid and invalid inputs.
• Model functions — tested against an instaconnect_test MySQL database.
• Coverage target: 80%+ for utils and validators.
• Every API endpoint tested: success, validation failure, auth failure, 404, permission cases.
• Separate test database seeded per suite.
• Critical flows: register → login → create post → like → comment → follow → edit profile → dark mode.
• Postman collection committed to repo; runnable via Newman in CI/CD.
• All pages at 320 px, 768 px, 1024 px, 1440 px viewports.
• All animations at 60 fps on Chrome and Safari.
• File upload: JPG / PNG / WEBP success; invalid types and oversized files rejected.
• Full flow on production deployment before release sign-off.
• All core features (Section 6.1) implemented and verified against user stories (Section 7).
• All API endpoints (Section 13) returning correct responses with correct status codes.
• Application deployed with a live demo URL and public GitHub repository.
• Application fully functional on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge (latest stable).
• Fully responsive at all breakpoints from 320 px to 1440 px+.
• Week 3 task submission accepted with passing evaluation score.
